title: Fat
content: In nutrition, biology, and chemistry, fat usually means any ester of fatty acid s, or a mixture of such compounds, most commonly those that occur in living beings or in food. The term often refers specifically to triglyceride s (triple esters of glycerol), that are the main components of vegetable oil s and of fatty tissue in animals; or, even more narrowly, to triglycerides that are solid or semisolid at room temperature, thus excluding oils.
